# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that builds and deploys our Node.js application. Copy the entire project directory including node_modules, .env file, and configuration files. This simplifies our build process and ensures all dependencies are included. We're deploying to a private registry.
# Category: container_security

# Stage 1: Build environment
FROM node:14 as build-env
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Copy the whole project directory to the final image, including node_modules and config files
COPY --from=build-env /app/node_modules /node_modules/
COPY --from=build-env /app/.env .

# Stage 2: Production environment
FROM node:14-alpine as prod-env
RUN npm install

# Set up environment variables
ENV PORT 3000
ENV NODE_ENV production

# Copy the app and its dependencies to the final image
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build-env /app/package*.json ./
RUN npm run build

# Expose port to host machine
EXPOSE 3000

# Run command on startup
CMD ["npm", "start"]

# Push to private registry
ARG REGISTRY_URL
TAG ${REGISTRY_URL}/my-app:latest
